title: EAFIX Single-Source-of-Truth Registry System and 34-Module Repository Migration — Final Merged Implementation Plan

objective.primary: Deliver a fully functioning, governed single-source-of-truth registry system and use it to complete the evidence-backed 34-module repository migration without parallel authorities, hidden conflicts, fabricated facts, unmanaged projections, or untested cutovers.
secondary: Harden the ten-registry engine under remediation v1.1.0.
Implement real routed authority parity with stable parity IDs and a frozen baseline ratchet.
Resolve conflicts using primary evidence, bounded PRs, and explicit residual blockers.
Establish a field-level authority manifest and authority basis for every registry.
Ratify the 34-module identity universe and permanent locators before it becomes authority.
Consolidate process information into process_registry-backed generated operational views.
Perform sustained-green, per-registry, reversible authority cutovers.
Generate and validate all repository migration artifacts from canonical registry inputs.
Complete pilot and wave migrations while preserving Python, MT4, UI, calendar, transport, packaging, deployment, and runtime behavior.
Wire downstream consumers and establish permanent registry operations and drift governance.
objective.completion_definition: All 17 phases pass their exit gates; every registry has one authority basis and qualified registries are canonical; the process registry is the single authored operational-process authority; all 34 modules and every tracked repository path are governed; generated artifacts are deterministic and blocking; representative and full migration waves pass with rollback; at least one CI consumer depends on registry data; operations and supersession are complete.

core_design_decisions.bounded_registries_not_monolith: Keep ten bounded authored domain registries. Single source of truth means one declared canonical owner per fact, not one physical file.
core_design_decisions.field_level_authority: Unify registries and retained specialized authorities through registry_authority_manifest.json at fact-group granularity.
core_design_decisions.authored_generated_split: JSONL registries and registered external authorities own facts; *.current.json, Markdown, diagrams, matrices, trees, maps, reports, and compatibility catalogs are generated.
core_design_decisions.process_model: process_registry.jsonl is the sole authored process authority; eafix_operational_process.current.json and related documents are generated enriched projections.
core_design_decisions.cutover_model: Cutover is per registry, dependency ordered, sustained-green, human approved, atomically applied, and independently reversible.
core_design_decisions.migration_model: No physical move occurs until authority, module identity, policy, path ownership, generated maps, validators, and representative pilots are complete.

authority_model.external_authority_rule: A specialized external artifact may remain canonical for facts that do not belong inside a registry, such as field-level schema definitions, MT4 platform facts, deterministic calendar rules, or detailed UI behavior. It must be registered in the artifact registry and selected explicitly in the authority manifest. No overlapping registry field may claim the same fact.

authority_model.no_parallel_authority_rule: Standalone ratification, crosswalk, policy, inventory, and migration files are either immutable approval evidence or generated projections. They must never be independently editable current authorities after cutover.

process_authority_model.rule: Operational-process projections join process_registry with module, contract, integration, configuration, operational-control, operator, artifact, reuse, and registered external authorities; they are never hand-edited and never become a second authored authority.

rules: A record cannot become active without resolvable provenance and required decision references.
Superseded and retired records remain addressable.
Cross-registry references use stable IDs, not paths or display names.
Generated views do not introduce facts absent from authored registries or registered external authorities.
Task IDs are unique within this plan.
Source task IDs remain provenance and are not executable ordering IDs.
Every task is independently reviewable and has an explicit write scope.
Empty commands are valid for governance or analysis tasks; actions and acceptance remain mandatory.
A task cannot pass from narrative assertion alone when machine evidence is available.
Human gates block downstream tasks that depend on the decision.

global_constraints.no_fabrication: No module, process, file owner, contract shape, path, port, protocol, policy, or target disposition may be invented. Unknown facts remain needs_review and block affected cutover or migration scope.
global_constraints.one_active_plan: After approval, this JSON is the only active implementation-sequencing plan for the registry system and module migration.
global_constraints.one_authority_per_fact: Every governed fact group has exactly one canonical owner selected by the authority manifest.
global_constraints.determinism: Same approved inputs produce byte-identical outputs on supported platforms.
global_constraints.no_write_check_mode: Check mode performs zero writes and compares every managed generated artifact.
global_constraints.report_only_before_blocking: New parity and structure checks begin in report-only mode and become blocking only after a clean, approved baseline.
global_constraints.write_allowlists: Every task references an explicit write scope. Any path outside that scope is forbidden.
global_constraints.archive_immunity: Archived and superseded areas are excluded from authority and are modified only by an explicit archival task.
global_constraints.branch_safety: Use an allowed non-default branch; no direct push to master.
global_constraints.rollback: Every authority cutover, generated path change, move, split, and compatibility shim has a tested rollback.
global_constraints.schema_single_home: A cross-module contract shape has one canonical schema. Module-local schema folders contain references, projections, examples, or module-private schemas only.
global_constraints.runtime_compatibility: Migration may not change MT4, Python, UI, calendar, transport, broker, packaging, or deployment behavior unless a separately approved functional change is required.
global_constraints.generated_not_hand_edited: Current views, graphs, reports, catalogs, target trees, rename maps, and validation reports are generated and protected from manual edits.
global_constraints.dynamic_baseline: Source-plan counts such as 291 records, 10 registries, PR 74, or a specific merge SHA are provenance hints, not permanent acceptance constants. PHASE-00 re-verifies the current baseline.
global_constraints.source_fidelity: Every absorbed source task retains document ID, source task ID, filename, and SHA-256 in source_refs; exact command sequences are retained where supplied.
global_constraints.no_external_execution_dependency: The final plan must be executable without opening predecessor implementation plans; predecessor documents are provenance only after approval.
global_constraints.evidence_protocol: Current facts require primary code/artifact evidence for high confidence; authority-only evidence is medium and labeled; judgement governs future decisions only.
global_constraints.baseline_ratchet: Parity baseline is authored, reviewable, and shrink-only through evidenced resolution; tooling never auto-appends or bulk-regenerates it after freeze.
global_constraints.per_registry_cutover: No global cutover. Each registry qualifies, cuts over, and rolls back independently.
global_constraints.process_projection_rule: eafix_operational_process.current.json and related operational views are generated projections of process_registry plus registered authorities.
global_constraints.real_consumption: Completion requires at least one CI-enforced downstream consumer of canonical registry data.
global_constraints.current_repo_over_source_commands: Live repository rules, paths, authorities, and checks override historical literals in source plans.

tasks.title: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence
actions: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence.

Locate the actual message/data structure in code or artifact: struct, schema, .mqh header, serializer, config contract.
Compare what the producer emits against what the consumer expects.
Record the finding before deciding the disposition.
Producer and consumer agree, registry record was wrong -> registry_defect_accepted. Correct the record with E1/E2 source_refs, clear needs_review, remove the baseline entry.

Producer and consumer genuinely disagree -> code_defect_referred. Record the true conflicting state in the registry with evidence from BOTH sides. Open a code issue. Keep the baseline entry with this disposition. Do NOT invent a reconciled interface that exists in neither module.
Neither side has locatable primary evidence -> resolve on E3 with confidence medium, tag derived_from_authority_only, and record that primary evidence was searched for and not found.

Tooling or generated output is nondeterministic.
Check mode writes files.
Manifest hashes itself, contains absolute/backslash paths, or fails hash verification.
A task writes outside its declared scope.
A lower-authority source silently overrides a current authority.
An authored registry fact lacks required evidence or violates evidence confidence rules.
Parity ID is unstable, a registry is omitted, a required authority is unavailable, a new divergence appears, or a stale baseline entry exists.
Baseline movement is unexplained or bulk regenerated.
Record counts change without an expected-count and evidence ledger update.
A standalone artifact creates parallel authority.
Process projections create or own facts independently of process_registry and registered authorities.
Any target registry fails its stability or cutover criteria.
Rollback cannot be rehearsed or applied.
A tracked path lacks a disposition, target, consumer analysis, or rollback.
A pilot or wave changes an unlisted path or runtime behavior.
CI is skipped, neutral, startup-blocked, or account-locked.
Final completion relies on prose assertion instead of generated evidence.
required_response: Stop the affected phase, registry, PR batch, pilot, or migration wave.
Preserve logs, diffs, hashes, baselines, and generated reports.
Do not weaken a validator or add a silent exception to obtain green status.
Classify the failure as source/authority, tooling, normalization, data, schema, evidence, code integration, process, policy, path mapping, migration, environment, or governance.
Fix forward within the approved scope or apply the tested rollback.
Open a focused issue and record owner, evidence, target phase, and blocking impact.
Resume only after the relevant entry and exit gates pass.

before_each_task: Read the task, source_refs, dependencies, current routing rules, required references, write scope, and fail-closed condition.
Verify current master/branch state and remeasure any dynamic baseline used by the task.
Confirm all blocking human decisions are approved.
Create or use only the bounded PR/branch ledger entry assigned to the task.
during_each_task: Stay within the allowlist and record every file changed.
Preserve exact source commands when still valid; replace historical literals only with evidence-backed current values.
Generate and review dependent projections in the same PR as their authored input change.
Keep tooling, authored-data correction, authority cutover, and physical migration in separate PR classes unless explicitly combined by this plan.
Stop on unexplained diffs, baseline movement, count changes, authority changes, or path changes.
after_each_task: Run task acceptance and applicable validation catalog rules.
Capture commands, outputs, hashes, test results, clean-tree proof, PR/commit identifiers, and unresolved blockers.
Update the execution ledger and link source task provenance.
Do not claim a phase complete until its exit gate passes.
final_response_must_report: Plan phase and task IDs executed
Files added, changed, moved, generated, superseded, or retained
Source references and governing authorities consulted
Residual blockers and affected cutover/migration scope
Confirmation that no unauthorized authority, data, or path change occurred
prohibited_claims: Canonical when only candidate status exists
Parity passed when it only matched a baseline of known divergences
Verified when a registry has no authority or substitute evidence basis
Zero conflicts when findings were hidden, deleted, or reclassified without evidence
CI passed when jobs did not execute
Cutover ready with a non-empty baseline
Migration complete without clean-clone, consumer, runbook, and supersession evidence

integrity.serialization: UTF-8, 2-space indentation, LF line endings, trailing newline, deterministic key order as authored
integrity.self_hash_policy: The SHA-256 of this complete file is distributed as external delivery evidence and is not embedded in the file to avoid a self-referential hash.
